skills.name: skill-management
skills.description: Browse, install, update, and contribute skills from the ouroboros-skills shared repository. This is the bootstrap skill that teaches agents how to manage their skill library.

skills.name: autopilot
skills.description: Operate as an engineer, not a journal-keeper. Removes human gates from full-delivery work: the agent ultrathinks, decides, ships, uses harsh sub-agent reviewer gates, keeps Arc/resume continuity current, and validates the explicit terminal state. Fires when the agent drafts action items, frames tractable failures as known quirks, overrides wrong defaults via flag, hot-patches without a source PR, stops a broken thing instead of investigating it, or tries to hand back an open PR as done. Only two narrow exceptions: human-only credential/capability, or unrecoverable destructive shared-production action.

skills.name: stay-in-turn
skills.description: Stay-in-turn for long-running work. Replaces the background+ScheduleWakeup+yield anti-pattern with a Monitor stream that produces events back into the same turn. Reach for this whenever the operator says 'do not return control until X' or whenever a task involves waiting on CI, a chain of merges, a build, or any operation longer than ~2 minutes.

skills.name: work-ideator
skills.description: Explore ambiguous product, architecture, workflow, or coding ideas before invoking work-planner. Use when the user asks to ideate, think through, investigate, flesh out, compare prior art, run Tinfoil Hat and Stranger With Candy scrutiny passes, or prepare a better planning handoff before $work-planner; especially for harness changes where naming, scope, mental model, or implementation shape is still unsettled.

skills.name: workbench-operator
skills.description: Use Ouro Workbench as the native control room for terminal/TUI agents, including boss-agent check-ins, Workbench MCP tools, Desk mirroring, onboarding, and first-class Claude Code, Codex, Copilot CLI, and shell sessions.

skills.name: work-planner
skills.description: Task planner for coding tasks. Generates planning docs, runs reviewer-gated approval by default, converts to doing docs after the correct gate clears, and treats autopilot/no-human-gates mandates as sub-agent reviewer gates rather than human stops.

skills.name: work-doer
skills.description: Executes doing.md units sequentially with strict TDD. Reads the doing doc, resolves ordinary blockers with sub-agent reviewers/fixers under autopilot, updates continuity state, and commits after each unit.

skills.name: work-merger
skills.description: Sync-and-merge agent. Fetches origin/main, resolves conflicts using task docs, creates PRs, runs harsh reviewer gates, waits for CI, merges to main, verifies terminal deploy/install state when applicable, and cleans up stale PR/branch/worktree state.

skills.name: video-editing
skills.description: Build and edit videos using Remotion with kinetic typography, VO-synced timing, and motion design. Use this skill when the user asks to create, edit, or fix video content.

skills.name: design
skills.description: Design and build production-grade frontend interfaces from scratch. Covers the full workflow from context gathering through visual direction, implementation, and polish.

skills.name: frontend-design
skills.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.

skills.name: seo-titles
skills.description: Write HTML title tags that rank AND get clicked. Marketing message first, SEO element second.

skills.name: book-fetch
skills.description: Search for ebooks on libgen, download EPUBs, and optionally deliver them to an e-reader or Calibre library. Infrastructure-agnostic — discovers the user's setup at runtime.

skills.name: inch-worm
skills.description: Open-ended codebase improvement loop. Start with one concrete seed issue, fix it, log side observations to a bundle task backlog, pick the next highest-value discovery, repeat, and close the backlog when done. Each fix is its own PR.

skills.name: full-systems-audit
skills.description: Perform a comprehensive codebase audit covering architecture, code quality, and modularization. Produces an audit report plus a routed backlog for work-planner, inch-worm, and companion skills.

skills.name: dragon-hunt
skills.description: Run an adversarial end-to-end bug hunt across product, backend, auth, data, integrations, MCP/agent surfaces, and deployment assumptions. Find, prove, fix, test, and log every reachable defect before declaring a system ready.

skills.name: word-docs
skills.description: Convert markdown drafts into shareable Word documents using a bundled helper script.
